assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md: CVE ID = 'CVE-2026-31812', Vulnerable library = 'quinn-proto', Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = '0.11.14'. All four fields are correctly extracted in the table on lines 7-13.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md line 29: 'Based on the Ecosystem Mappings in the security matrix, the ecosystem is **Cargo**. The lock file is Cargo.lock and the check command is git show <tag>:Cargo.lock.'

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: The security-matrix-mock.md defines 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (stream 2.1.x) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (stream 2.2.x). The version-impact.md Combined Impact Summary table (lines 33-41) lists all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4. No versions are skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, each version uses the pinned build tag from the supportability matrix: 2.1.0 -> v0.3.8, 2.1.1 -> v0.3.12, 2.2.0 -> v0.4.5, 2.2.1 -> v0.4.8, 2.2.2 -> v0.4.9, 2.2.3 -> v0.4.11, 2.2.4 -> v0.4.12. These match exactly the Build column tags in the security-matrix-mock.md supportability matrix.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md line 27: version 2.2.2 with tag v0.4.9 shows quinn-proto as '--' (no independent lookup), Affected = 'YES', and Notes = 'retag of 2.2.1 (same as 2.2.1; v0.4.9 = retag of v0.4.8, carries forward 0.11.12)'. The retag is identified and status carried forward from 2.2.1.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md: Current PSIRT-assigned version 'RHTPA 2.0.0' is shown (line 5). Proposed correction shows 'Remove: RHTPA 2.0.0' (line 25) and 'Add: RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2' (line 27). The diff between old and new values is explicit, with rationale explaining the PSIRT version is incorrect and lock-file analysis determines the correct affected versions.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md lines 43-48, the proposed Jira mutation uses version names: {"name": "RHTPA 2.2.0"}, {"name": "RHTPA 2.2.1"}, {"name": "RHTPA 2.2.2"}. No hardcoded Jira version IDs are used anywhere in the file.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md: Task 1 (upstream backport) includes Repository: 'backend' (line 36), Target Branch: 'release/0.4.z' (line 40), Acceptance Criteria (lines 69-72 with checkboxes), and Dependencies: 'Depends on: TC-8001' (line 79). Task 2 (downstream propagation) includes Repository: 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 101), Target Branch: 'main' (line 105), Acceptance Criteria (lines 126-128), and Dependencies including 'Depends on: upstream backport task (upstream backport must merge first)' (line 132). Linkage between tasks shown in lines 158-164 with a Blocks dependency.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md: Two tasks are created — Task 1: 'Upstream Backport (2.2.x stream)' (line 19) and Task 2: 'Downstream Propagation (2.2.x stream)' (line 83). The Blocks dependency is specified in lines 158-164: jira.create_link with inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: 'Blocks'. Line 12 explicitly states: 'Two tasks per affected stream -- upstream backport + downstream propagation with Blocks dependency'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md: Section titled 'Proposed Correction' (line 23), 'Proposed Jira Mutation' (line 39), and line 52: 'This mutation requires engineer confirmation before execution.' In remediation.md: Section titled 'Jira Linkage (proposed)' (line 139), line 193: 'The following actions would be performed after engineer confirmation:' followed by proposed label addition, comment posting, and task creation. All mutations are framed as proposals requiring confirmation, not as executed actions.
